commit.message:
Fix timeout and error rendering for background thread reliability

- Set socket.setdefaulttimeout(10) globally at startup — caps DNS+TCP
  on all platforms, no per-request dance needed
- Simplify do_lookup: no nested thread, rely on socket timeout directly
- Errors now render inside results_view (same code path as success)
  instead of the error_row container, because Flet reliably propagates
  page.update() when results_view.controls changes
- Add _showing_error flag so tab switch doesn't snapshot error widget
  into search_controls (prior good results are preserved)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: 5 bugs found during full integration test

1. Export: trigger expressions were internal Zabbix function IDs
   e.g. {48378} > 90 instead of avg(/template/key,5m) > 90
   Fix: fetch triggers via trigger.get with expandExpression:true
   instead of via template.get selectTriggers.
   Same fix applied to get_trigger_prototypes.

2. Export: exact template name not preferred over partial matches
   'nginx' matched 5 templates instead of the zbx-managed 'nginx'.
   Fix: find_templates tries filter:{host:[search]} exact match first;
   only falls back to partial search if no exact hit found.

3. Export: trigger_prototypes missing from discovery rule YAML output
   LLD rules with trigger prototypes (e.g. S3 alerts) exported without
   their trigger_prototypes block.
   Fix: export_template_raw fetches trigger_prototypes per rule;
   _raw_to_template builds TriggerPrototype objects; _rule_dict
   serializes them; _trig_proto_dict helper added.

4. Plan: no warning when dependent item prototype references a
   master_item_key that does not exist in the same LLD rule.
   Silent during plan, silently skipped during apply.
   Fix: _diff_discovery_rules validates master_item_key against
   non-dependent prototype keys in the same rule; emits warning
   into TemplateDiff.warnings; formatter prints them in yellow.
   Also surfaces warnings for brand-new templates (current=None path).

5. scripts/getS3Storage.py deleted by remote commit during rebase
   Commit 73910fd (Delete scripts/getS3Storage.py) on origin/main
   was applied over our add. Restored from commit b634471.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt BOM sort warning to Copilot instructions (#34327)

<!-- Please let the below note in for people that find this PR -->
> [!NOTE]
> Are you waiting for the changes in this PR to be merged?
> It would be very helpful if you could [test the resulting
artifacts](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki/Testing-PR-Builds) from
this PR and let us know in a comment if this change resolves your issue.
Thank you!

## Description

PR #34320 fixed RS0017 analyzer errors caused by `#nullable enable`
being sorted to the bottom of 14 Maps `PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt` files.
The root cause was a prior Copilot agent session that used `LC_ALL=C
sort -u` to resolve merge conflicts — the BOM bytes (`0xEF 0xBB 0xBF`)
sort after all ASCII characters, pushing the directive below the API
entries.

This updates the Copilot instructions to prevent this from recurring:

- Explains that `#nullable enable` must remain on line 1
- Warns against using plain `sort` on these files (BOM sort ordering)
- Provides a safe conflict resolution script that preserves the header
before sorting API entries

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: use staging directory for ACR builds (~9MB vs ~180MB upload)

az acr build's tar packer recursively walks ALL directories even when
excluded by .dockerignore (only .venv is in its hardcoded skip list).
With 558MB node_modules, this caused extremely slow uploads.

Fix: create a minimal staging directory with only the files referenced
by Dockerfile.api and Dockerfile.worker, then use that as build context.

commit.message:
fix: pass document_url to citation path builder for inline [N] badges

renderCitation() was calling getCitationFilePath(detail.reference) without
the documentUrl, so inline citation clicks resolved to the document_title
(e.g. 'purchase contract') instead of the blob filename with extension
(e.g. 'PURCHASE CONTRACT.pdf'), causing 'Content not found' 404 errors.

- Add documentUrl field to CitationDetail type
- Store sc.document_url in Strategy 1 citation details
- Pass detail.documentUrl to getCitationFilePath() in renderCitation()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: agent deploy sudo handling for localhost

Three bugs fixed in agent deploy:

1. _read_path caught OSError (including FileNotFoundError) and fell
   through to 'sudo cat' even for missing files, triggering sudo
   password prompt during diff of non-existent conf files.
   Fix: check p.exists() first; only try sudo on PermissionError.

2. _write_text embedded multiline content in the shell command string.
   Combining this with 'echo password | sudo -S' caused unterminated
   quoted string errors.
   Fix: write content to a tempfile, then sudo cp to destination.

3. agent deploy did not prompt for sudo password before attempting
   writes, causing interactive sudo prompt mid-flight.
   Fix: prompt for sudo password upfront (hidden input) when deploying
   locally with sudo enabled; pass via sudo -S to all subprocess calls.

Also reverted inventory.yaml IP back to 127.0.0.1 (zabbixtest3100 is
running on localhost).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(ipc): deep tests for transport, feature negotiation, and service communication (#142)

* test(ipc): add deep tests for transport, feature negotiation, and service communication

Add 80 new tests in ipc_transport_negotiation_tests.rs covering areas
not exercised by existing test files:

1. Feature negotiation edge cases (10 tests):
   - Re-negotiation registering multiple clients
   - Feature ordering independence
   - Duplicate feature handling
   - All 7 server features activation
   - Incompatible client behavior (not registered, still gets supported list)
   - Unicode and many unknown features
   - Timeout config and min_client_version in result

2. Transport layer behavior (14 tests):
   - Buffer size defaults and customization
   - Serde roundtrip for transport/IPC configs
   - Connection limit and timeout boundary values
   - Named pipe with long names, TCP with port 0 and IPv6
   - Server restart cycle preserving config

3. Message serialization for all 8 message types (11 tests):
   - Frame roundtrip (header+payload) for DEVICE, PROFILE, SAFETY,
     HEALTH, FEATURE_NEGOTIATION, GAME, TELEMETRY, DIAGNOSTIC
   - Multiple frames in stream buffer parsing
   - Error response frames with combined flags
   - All flags combined in single header

4. Error propagation across IPC boundary (14 tests):
   - IO error conversions (broken pipe, refused, timed out, would block)
   - Error message preservation for all error variants
   - Send+Sync trait verification
   - Debug format non-emptiness for all 13 variants
   - Codec edge cases (empty message, garbage bytes)
   - Server double-start returns InvalidConfig

5. Concurrent client handling (6 tests):
   - 50 concurrent negotiations
   - Interleaved register/unregister (20 clients)
   - Health broadcast with 10 subscribers
   - Concurrent negotiate and stop
   - Unregister nonexistent/double-unregister

6. Large message handling (7 tests):
   - Encode at/under/over size limits
   - Exact boundary and one-byte-over
   - 64KB payload roundtrip via frame
   - encode_to_buffer clears previous content
   - encoded_len matches actual encoding

7. Proptest for message framing (4 property tests, 128 cases each):
   - Framed stream roundtrip with varying payloads
   - Header with arbitrary payload_len roundtrips
   - Version compatibility reflexive property
   - Higher minor always compatible, different major never compatible

8. Health event edge cases (5 tests):
   - Broadcast with no subscribers
   - Subscriber dropped doesn't affect others
   - Large metadata (100 entries)
   - Contiguous repr values
   - Rapid burst of 100 events

9. Server/client info edge cases (7 tests):
   - Connected clients info correctness
   - Same-ID registration overwrites
   - Features stored from negotiation
   - Default config values
   - Fresh server state

All tests return Result (no unwrap/expect), following project conventions.

Add deep integration tests for both Simucube and Cammus HID protocol crates:

hid-simucube-protocol (57 tests):
- PIDFF effect encoding roundtrips (set_effect, constant_force, periodic,
  envelope, condition, ramp_force, effect_operation, block_free,
  device_control, device_gain, create_new_effect)
- Block load report parsing (success, full, error, short buffer)
- Output report wire-level byte encoding verification
- Extended input report diagnostics (angle, speed, torque, faults, status)
- HID joystick report all-axes/all-buttons coverage
- VID/PID cross-consistency and uniqueness checks
- Error display formatting
- WheelCapabilities cross-model consistency
- Proptest fuzzing (parse never panics, rejects short, roundtrips,
  steering range bounds, PIDFF roundtrips)

racing-wheel-hid-cammus-protocol (53 tests):
- PIDFF effect encoding roundtrips (all encoder functions)
- Input report field isolation (clutch, handbrake, mid-range, i16::MIN)
- Direct torque wire-level byte verification
- VID/PID cross-consistency (wheel vs pedal PID ranges, model/name match)
- Error formatting and equality
- Report constant cross-checks
- PIDFF effect type coverage via re-export
- Proptest fuzzing (parse never panics, rejects short, all axes clamped,
  steering sign, torque structure/saturation, buttons/throttle roundtrip)

All tests return Result (no unwrap/expect per project rules).

commit.message:
fix: LogViewer reopen (LockOSThread) + DB corruption auto-repair

LogViewer: Added runtime.LockOSThread() to the LogViewer goroutine,
matching the pattern used by the main window (ui_main.go:169).
Without it, WebView2's COM message loop breaks when Go reschedules
the goroutine to a different OS thread — causing Run() to never
return on window close, permanently blocking reopening.

DB corruption: Added repairDBIfNeeded() to initHistoryDB(). Runs
PRAGMA integrity_check(1) on startup and rebuilds the FTS5 index
if corruption is detected. This fixes 'database disk image is
malformed' errors that prevented delete/merge operations when
the FTS5 external-content table was out of sync.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix timeout: use socket.setdefaulttimeout(8) + 10s thread join

requests timeout= alone doesn't cap DNS resolution on Windows.
socket.setdefaulttimeout(8) applies to all socket ops (DNS+TCP+read)
and is restored after the request. Thread join reduced to 10s as the
hard cutoff that fires regardless.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove all vim.notify from auto-load async callbacks

vim.notify in async job callbacks (even inside vim.schedule) can crash
when the user has noice.nvim or nvim-notify installed, because those
plugins replace vim.notify with a function that creates floating windows
and may not be safe at the exact moment the callback fires.

Changes:
- Auto-load (non-user-initiated) is now completely silent: no INFO
  notifications are emitted during background comment loading
- Only ERROR-level messages and user-initiated (:GitCommentsLoad)
  actions produce notifications
- Removed messy nested pcall structure; replaced with clean linear
  callback chain with a single pcall around the store/sign-refresh step
- Added a status table (per repo root) to track loading state
- nvim_buf_is_valid check added before nvim_buf_is_loaded
